title: rhus thezera
intro: Rhus thezera, also known as poison ivy, oak, or sumac, is a plant that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to treat various skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rhus thezera include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Rhus thezera include tinctures, salves, and ointments, which can be applied topically to treat skin conditions or ingested in small amounts under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
